figures.caption: Table 3: Performance comparison of the two baselines across different subsets on GenState-AI. Best results are highlighted in bold.
Figure 3: Relative-order histogram for all groups combined (195 queries total) under VCF-Lik. This provides an overall view of the model’s ordering preferences across the entire dataset.

Figure 4: Comparison of relative-order distributions across subsets under VCF-Lik. The top panel shows absolute counts, while the bottom panel shows percentages, facilitating cross-subset analysis of ordering patterns.

Figure 5: Typical failure cases observed under VCF-Lik on GenState-AI. The left example shows a semantic substitution distracting the model, while the right example highlights the more common temporal confusion where a temporally plausible but end-state-incorrect video is ranked above the true match.

Figure 2: Qualitative examples of generated videos and their hard negatives. For each prompt, we show the original generated video (top), semantic hard negative (middle), and temporal hard negative (bottom). Each image represents a temporal sequence of frames horizontally concatenated for visualization.
